description: Expand the underfilled README core sections Theories (5→≥8), Coordination Patterns (4→≥8), and Frameworks (5→≥8) with high-signal, taxonomy-aligned entries that preserve scope discipline, alphabetization, canonical URLs, and agent-factory relevance keywords; pass make check, make test, and git diff --check.

context.customerAsk: Phase 7 content expansion: extend the underfilled README core sections without weakening scope discipline. Expand Theories from 5 entries to at least 8, Coordination Patterns from 4 entries to at least 8, and Frameworks from 5 entries to at least 8. Add only high-signal resources that clearly fit docs/taxonomy.md and CONTRIBUTING.md, keep entries alphabetized, use canonical stable URLs where possible, avoid duplicates, and keep descriptions factual and explicitly tied to coordination, orchestration, delegation, routing, handoffs, shared state, or group-level evaluation.
context.problem: The three core README sections that anchor agent-factory literacy—Theories, Coordination Patterns, and Frameworks—remain thin relative to other Phase 7 sections. Readers and contributors lack enough on-main exemplars to compare conceptual models, coordination topologies, and orchestration frameworks. Underfilled core sections weaken scope enforcement because borderline submissions have fewer high-quality reference entries per lane.
context.solution: Add curated entries only to the three target README sections until each meets the minimum count bar, following docs/taxonomy.md include rules and CONTRIBUTING.md formatting and relevance rules. Preserve governance prose and all non-target curated sections. Verify make check, make test, and git diff --check pass before merge.

acceptanceCriteria: README Theories contains at least 8 resource entries (currently 5) using - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. format with descriptions ending in a period and entries alphabetized by link text
README Coordination Patterns contains at least 8 resource entries (currently 4) using the same format, tone, and alphabetization rules
README Frameworks contains at least 8 resource entries (currently 5) using the same format, tone, and alphabetization rules

Every new entry uses a stable canonical URL, fits docs/taxonomy.md include rules for its section, includes an agent-factory scope keyword in the description, and introduces no duplicate URLs anywhere in README.md
README governance sections (Scope, Contributing, Community) and all non-target curated sections remain unchanged except unavoidable whitespace or merge hygiene
Quality gate: make check, make test, and git diff --check all pass from the repository root

userStories.title: Expand Theories section to minimum density
userStories.description: As a reader designing agent societies, I want more foundational theories indexed on main so I can compare enduring conceptual models for group organization, cooperation, and planning beyond the current five seed entries.
acceptanceCriteria: README Theories contains at least 8 entries below the section intro (at least 3 new entries beyond the existing five seed entries)
Each new entry is a foundational idea for organizing agent groups per docs/taxonomy.md Theories include rules—not a runnable SDK or pattern-only topology writeup
Each entry uses exact format - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. with a factual one-sentence description ending in a period and an explicit agent-factory scope keyword
Entries are alphabetized by link text across the full section and no duplicate URLs are introduced anywhere in README.md
make check passes after Theories expansion
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expand Coordination Patterns section to minimum density
userStories.description: As a system architect choosing multi-agent topologies, I want more pattern references on main so I can study distinct orchestration shapes beyond the current four seed entries.
acceptanceCriteria: README Coordination Patterns contains at least 8 entries below the section intro (at least 4 new entries beyond the existing four seed entries)
Each new entry documents a reusable multi-agent topology per docs/taxonomy.md Coordination Patterns include rules—not a framework product page whose primary value is the software itself
Each entry uses exact format - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. with a factual one-sentence description ending in a period and an explicit agent-factory scope keyword
New URLs do not duplicate URLs already present elsewhere in README.md and entries are alphabetized by link text across the full section
make check passes after Coordination Patterns expansion
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expand Frameworks section to minimum density
userStories.description: As a builder selecting orchestration software, I want more multi-agent frameworks indexed on main so I can compare distinct runtimes for delegation, handoffs, and group coordination beyond the current five seed entries.
acceptanceCriteria: README Frameworks contains at least 8 entries below the section intro (at least 3 new entries beyond the existing five seed entries)

Each new entry is software whose core purpose is multi-agent coordination, orchestration, routing, or handoffs per docs/taxonomy.md Frameworks include rules—not a generic LLM SDK, single-agent chatbot framework, prompt library, or example-only repository already listed under Examples and Templates
Each entry uses exact format - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. with a factual one-sentence description ending in a period emphasizing orchestration, delegation, handoffs, or group coordination
New framework URLs point to canonical project homes and do not duplicate URLs already in README.md; entries are alphabetized by link text across the full section
make check passes after Frameworks expansion
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Verify batch quality gates and section integrity
userStories.description: As a maintainer merging this batch, I want end-to-end verification that expanded core sections satisfy automated checks and leave unrelated repository content untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: From repository root, make check exits 0
From repository root, make test exits 0
git diff --check reports no whitespace errors on changed files
README Theories, Coordination Patterns, and Frameworks each contain at least 8 entries with at least 10 combined new entries relative to pre-batch counts
README Scope, Contributing, and Community sections remain present and unweakened; non-target curated sections contain no unintended edits
Changed content files are limited to README.md and planning artifacts for this batch
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
